basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Ghāfir — “The Forgiver” — opens with the letters Ḥā-Mīm and at once unfolds the tension of the divine Names: Forgiver of sin, Acceptor of repentance, Severe in punishment, Lord of bounty. Qushayrī reads in it a pedagogy of hope: when God frightens His servants with one Name, He at once overtakes their hearts with two Names of glad tidings — Forgiver for the one who sinned, Acceptor of repentance for the one who repented. The sūra brings forth the angels who bear the Throne and ask forgiveness for the believers, then the great account of Moses against Pharaoh, Hāmān, and Korah. At its heart stands a moving figure: the believer of Pharaoh's household, who concealed his faith and counseled his people with tenacious fidelity, until he entrusted his fate to God — “I entrust my affair to God” — and was preserved from the machinations. It culminates in the promise of invocation: “Call upon Me, I will respond to you,” from which Qushayrī draws a tender litany: call upon Me without any distraction, I will respond without any delay; call upon Me with contrition, I will respond with favor. It warns of the Day of the Imminent, when hearts rise to the throat, and is sealed on the immutable sunna: faith wrung out by terror, at the sight of the punishment, no longer avails.
themes: the Forgiver and the balance of the Names
the angels who bear the Throne
the hidden believer of Pharaoh's family
“Call upon Me, I will respond to you”

versets.ar: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ حم
versets.tr: bismi llāhi r-raḥmāni r-raḥīmi · ḥā mīm
versets.trad: Ḥā. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī offers several keys to these two letters. Ḥā-Mīm: an affair has now been decreed and accomplished (ḥumma l-amr). By another reading, the Ḥā points to His clemency (ḥilm) and the Mīm to His majesty (majd): by My clemency and My majesty, I do not consign forever to the Fire one who believes in Me. And it is also said that these letters are the keys of His Names — a ciphered threshold where the Word veils itself the better to point to the mystery of the Name.

mots.def: two opening letters; keys of the divine Names per Qushayrī.

versets.ar: تَنزِيلُ الْكِتَابِ مِنَ اللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ
versets.tr: tanzīlu l-kitābi mina llāhi l-ʿazīzi l-ʿalīmi
versets.trad: The sending down of the Book is from God, the Mighty, the All-Knowing,
versets.commentaire: The sending down of the Book (tanzīl al-kitāb) proceeds from God alone. Qushayrī illumines the two Names: al-ʿAzīz, the One who makes His friends mighty (al-muʿizz li-awliyāʾihi); al-ʿAlīm, who knows what has been and will be of them — and His knowledge of their past does not prevent Him from carrying out His decree. Glory and knowledge are joined in the source from which the Word flows.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب
mots.sens: the sending down of the Book
mots.def: tanzīl: gradual revelation, causing to descend.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ع ل م
mots.def: ʿazīz: mighty, who exalts; ʿalīm: all-knowing.

versets.ar: غَافِرِ الذَّنبِ وَقَابِلِ التَّوْبِ شَدِيدِ الْعِقَابِ ذِي الطَّوْلِ ۖ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ إِلَيْهِ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: ghāfiri dh-dhanbi wa-qābili t-tawbi shadīdi l-ʿiqābi dhī ṭ-ṭawli lā ilāha illā huwa ilayhi l-maṣīru
versets.trad: Forgiver of sin, Acceptor of repentance, Severe in punishment, Lord of bounty. There is no god but He; to Him is the homecoming.
versets.commentaire: The pivotal verse of the sūra. Qushayrī observes that God knows the sinner whose heart is broken, and He first lifts that brokenness by presenting His forgiveness: He places the Name “Forgiver of sin” before “Acceptor of repentance,” to calm souls with two Names that summon hope. Then comes “Severe in punishment” — at once counterbalanced by “Lord of bounty” (dhū ṭ-ṭawl). Such is God's way: when He frightens His servants with one Name, He overtakes their hearts with two Names of glad tidings. He distributes further: “Forgiver” for the one who persisted and sinned, “Acceptor of repentance” for the one who acknowledged and grieved, “Severe” for the one who denied, “Lord of bounty” for the one who knew and professed Unity. And since “to Him is the homecoming” (al-maṣīr), the journeying (al-masīr) toward Him becomes sweet.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ذ ن ب
mots.def: ghāfir: who covers, forgives; dhanb: sin.

mots.racine: ق ب ل / ت و ب
mots.def: qābil: who receives; tawb: turning back, repentance.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ق ب

versets.ar: مَا يُجَادِلُ فِي آيَاتِ اللَّهِ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَلَا يَغْرُرْكَ تَقَلُّبُهُمْ فِي الْبِلَادِ
versets.tr: mā yujādilu fī āyāti llāhi illā lladhīna kafarū fa-lā yaghrurka taqallubuhum fī l-bilādi
versets.trad: None dispute over the signs of God but those who disbelieve; so let not their comings and goings through the land deceive you.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the proof appears and the evidence shines forth, lucid minds yield to faith. But frozen hearts persist: the baleful idolatry they carry raises a veil between them and fairness. He extends the warning to those who do not honor the friends of God, who contest their charisms and dispute what God favors His servants with: they cannot tell their own excess from their lack, and will often be put to shame. The thriving bustle of the deniers is but a respite, not a sign of election.

versets.ar: كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَالْأَحْزَابُ مِن بَعْدِهِمْ ۖ وَهَمَّت كُلُّ أُمَّةٍ بِرَسُولِهِمْ لِيَأْخُذُوهُ ۖ وَجَادَلُوا بِالْبَاطِلِ لِيُدْحِضُوا بِهِ الْحَقَّ فَأَخَذْتُهُمْ ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عِقَابِ
versets.tr: kadhdhabat qablahum qawmu nūḥin wa-l-aḥzābu min baʿdihim wa-hammat kullu ummatin bi-rasūlihim li-yaʾkhudhūhu wa-jādalū bi-l-bāṭili li-yudḥiḍū bihi l-ḥaqqa fa-akhadhtuhum fa-kayfa kāna ʿiqābi
versets.trad: Before them the people of Noah denied, and the factions after them; every community plotted against their messenger to seize him, and they disputed with falsehood to refute the truth thereby. So I seized them — and how was My punishment!
versets.commentaire: The denial of the prophets was the constant practice of vanished peoples. Qushayrī warns the contemplative: whoever denies this Path makes a religion of his denial, thinks he draws near to God by his rejection, and counts his slander against the friends of God among his good deeds; but God chastises him here below by leaving him to his state, turning his heart away from these realities and depriving him of them. The seizing is not only future: it is already the dryness of the closed heart.

mots.def: adḥaḍa: to make totter, refute the truth.

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّهُمْ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika ḥaqqat kalimatu rabbika ʿalā lladhīna kafarū annahum aṣḥābu n-nāri
versets.trad: And thus the word of your Lord was realized against those who disbelieved: that they are the companions of the Fire.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when God's decree seals a servant in misfortune, the abundance of counsel poured upon him avails nothing — God prevails over His command. Whoever is gripped by the hand of perdition, no effort, no intercession can wrest him from its claws. A grave word, returning the servant to humility and supplication rather than to presumption about his own destiny.

mots.def: ḥaqqa: to come true, become due.

mots.racine: ك ل م / ر ب ب

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَحْمِلُونَ الْعَرْشَ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهُ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَيُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا رَبَّنَا وَسِعْتَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ رَّحْمَةً وَعِلْمًا فَاغْفِرْ لِلَّذِينَ تَابُوا وَاتَّبَعُوا سَبِيلَكَ وَقِهِمْ عَذَابَ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: alladhīna yaḥmilūna l-ʿarsha wa-man ḥawlahu yusabbiḥūna bi-ḥamdi rabbihim wa-yuʾminūna bihi wa-yastaghfirūna li-lladhīna āmanū rabbanā wasiʿta kulla shayʾin raḥmatan wa-ʿilman fa-ghfir li-lladhīna tābū wa-ttabaʿū sabīlaka wa-qihim ʿadhāba l-jaḥīmi
versets.trad: Those who bear the Throne and those around it glorify the praise of their Lord, believe in Him, and ask forgiveness for those who believe: “Our Lord, You encompass all things in mercy and knowledge; so forgive those who repent and follow Your way, and shield them from the punishment of the Blaze.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the bearers of the Throne and those around it are among the elite of the angels; they are charged with glorification, then with seeking forgiveness for sinners — for the forgiveness of sin and repentance can only be conceived in relation to sin. They strive in prayer for them: they ask first for salvation, then for the raising of ranks, and refer the whole matter to God's mercy. A marvel: the highest creatures intercede for the weakest, and ground their prayer not on merit but on the infinite embrace of the Raḥma.

mots.racine: ح م ل / ع ر ش
mots.def: ʿarsh: divine Throne; ḥamala: to bear.

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ الَّتِي وَعَدتَّهُمْ وَمَن صَلَحَ مِنْ آبَائِهِمْ وَأَزْوَاجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: rabbanā wa-adkhilhum jannāti ʿadnin allatī waʿadtahum wa-man ṣalaḥa min ābāʾihim wa-azwājihim wa-dhurriyyātihim innaka anta l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: Our Lord, and admit them into the Gardens of Eden which You have promised them, and whoever was righteous among their fathers, their spouses, and their descendants. You are the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Continuing the angelic intercession, Qushayrī notes that the angels are not content with salvation: they ask for the raising of ranks, and extend their prayer to the righteous kin of the believers, so that the joy of reunion may perfect the felicity. The request is sealed on two Names: the Might that fulfills what it promises, and the Wisdom that sets each one in his rightful station.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع د ن

versets.ar: وَقِهِمُ السَّيِّئَاتِ ۚ وَمَن تَقِ السَّيِّئَاتِ يَوْمَئِذٍ فَقَدْ رَحِمْتَهُ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-qihimu s-sayyiʾāti wa-man taqi s-sayyiʾāti yawmaʾidhin fa-qad raḥimtahu wa-dhālika huwa l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīmu
versets.trad: And shield them from evil deeds; for whomever You shield from evil deeds that Day, You have indeed shown mercy. And that is the supreme triumph.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds a tender consolation: if God has permitted the basest of His creatures — the devils — to rage against you, He has also raised up, through intercession, the noblest of His creatures, the angels brought near, to pray on your behalf. To be shielded from evil on the dread Day is already to receive mercy: such, the verse says, is the immense triumph, where the divine guarding shows itself to be pure grace.

mots.racine: و ق ي / س و أ
mots.def: waqā: to protect; sayyiʾāt: evil deeds/punishments.

mots.racine: ف و ز / ع ظ م

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يُنَادَوْنَ لَمَقْتُ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ مِن مَّقْتِكُمْ أَنفُسَكُمْ إِذْ تُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى الْإِيمَانِ فَتَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū yunādawna la-maqtu llāhi akbaru min maqtikum anfusakum idh tudʿawna ilā l-īmāni fa-takfurūna
versets.trad: Those who disbelieved will be called out to: “God's loathing of you is greater than your loathing of yourselves, when you were called to faith and you disbelieved.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the harshest of the chastisements the Real inflicts are the traces of His wrath; and the sublimest of blessings, the traces of His good pleasure. When the denier recognizes, in the other world, that his Lord is angry with him, nothing is more crushing to his heart: he knows then that no weeping profits him, that no toil dispels his state, that no plea is heard and no stratagem hoped for. Self-loathing comes too late, once the door of return has closed.

versets.ar: قَالُوا رَبَّنَا أَمَتَّنَا اثْنَتَيْنِ وَأَحْيَيْتَنَا اثْنَتَيْنِ فَاعْتَرَفْنَا بِذُنُوبِنَا فَهَلْ إِلَىٰ خُرُوجٍ مِّن سَبِيلٍ
versets.tr: qālū rabbanā amattanā thnatayni wa-aḥyaytanā thnatayni fa-ʿtarafnā bi-dhunūbinā fa-hal ilā khurūjin min sabīlin
versets.trad: They will say: “Our Lord, You caused us to die twice and gave us life twice; now we acknowledge our sins. Is there any way to a way out?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī expounds the deniers' double death and double rebirth, then opens a window of rare beauty upon the lovers: the death of those people is circumscribed; but the people of love have in every instant a death and a life. He cites the verse: “I die when I lose You, then I revive — how many times I revive through You, how many times I die!” For the Real ceaselessly makes His elect oscillate between extinction (fanāʾ) and subsistence (baqāʾ), life and death, effacement and confirmation. The confession of the damned, by contrast, comes at the hour when confession no longer avails.

mots.sens: You caused us to die twice

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكُم بِأَنَّهُ إِذَا دُعِيَ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ كَفَرْتُمْ ۖ وَإِن يُشْرَكْ بِهِ تُؤْمِنُوا ۚ فَالْحُكْمُ لِلَّهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْكَبِيرِ
versets.tr: dhālikum bi-annahu idhā duʿiya llāhu waḥdahu kafartum wa-in yushrak bihi tuʾminū fa-l-ḥukmu li-llāhi l-ʿaliyyi l-kabīri
versets.trad: That is because when God alone was invoked, you disbelieved; but if partners were ascribed to Him, you believed. So the judgment belongs to God, the Most High, the Most Great.
versets.commentaire: Their perversity was to credit the idolaters and deny the divine Oneness. Qushayrī then lifts the gaze: the extinction of the deniers is bounded; but the people of love have in every breath a death and a life, effacement and confirmation. Judgment belongs to the “Most High, Most Great” alone: no authority besides Him decides, and it is in this absolute sovereignty that the servant finds, not dread, but the repose of total surrender.

mots.racine: د ع و / و ح د
mots.def: waḥdahu: He alone, in His oneness.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي يُرِيكُمْ آيَاتِهِ وَيُنَزِّلُ لَكُم مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ رِزْقًا ۚ وَمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ إِلَّا مَن يُنِيبُ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī yurīkum āyātihi wa-yunazzilu lakum mina s-samāʾi rizqan wa-mā yatadhakkaru illā man yunību
versets.trad: It is He who shows you His signs and sends down for you provision from the sky. Yet none takes heed but the one who turns back to Him.
versets.commentaire: A sumptuous page of Qushayrī. He shows them the signs of His grace in His gentleness, the signs of His coercion in His unveiling, the signs of His pardon when they declare their innocence, of His generosity when they implore, of His majesty when they fear and absent themselves, of His beauty when they return and respond. And the provision sent from the sky is threefold: for bodies, the grace of spiritual struggles; for hearts, the reality of contemplations; for inmost secrets, the modes of unions and increase. As for the one “who turns back” (man yunīb), he is the one who returns from habit to worship, from doubt to certainty, from creature to the Real, from ignorance to knowledge, from unknowing to gnosis.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / أ ي ي
mots.def: arā: to cause to see; āyāt: signs.
anāba: to turn to God, return contrite.

versets.ar: فَادْعُوا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-dʿū llāha mukhliṣīna lahu d-dīna wa-law kariha l-kāfirūna
versets.trad: So call upon God, devoting religion purely to Him, even though the disbelievers be averse.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a treatise on invocation. The condition of prayer is first to know the One you call upon; then to ask for what you truly need; then to seek nothing that contravenes His command, to withdraw from base and worldly things, and to be content with what your Master chooses for you. Sincerity (ikhlāṣ) consists in seeing the answer to come from Him alone, in claiming no merit for yourself save by His grace, and in knowing that if you become absorbed in your request — which is your share — you must not neglect the worship of your Lord, which is His right; for prayer is the marrow of worship.

mots.racine: خ ل ص / د ي ن

versets.ar: رَفِيعُ الدَّرَجَاتِ ذُو الْعَرْشِ يُلْقِي الرُّوحَ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ لِيُنذِرَ يَوْمَ التَّلَاقِ
versets.tr: rafīʿu d-darajāti dhū l-ʿarshi yulqī r-rūḥa min amrihi ʿalā man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi li-yundhira yawma t-talāqi
versets.trad: Exalter of ranks, Lord of the Throne, He casts the Spirit of His command upon whom He wills among His servants, to warn of the Day of Meeting,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī beautifully grades the exaltation: He raises the sinners to salvation, the obedient to rewards, the pure and the friends to charisms, the needy to sufficiency, the gnostics by piercing for them all the modes of the divine will. Higher still: the ranks of the obedient are in their bodies in Paradise, the ranks of the gnostics in their hearts here below — their gaze rises above the two worlds without settling in them; as for the lovers, their ranks rise to the point of seeking nothing, here or hereafter, but the good pleasure of their Beloved. The Spirit cast is by turns a spirit of inspiration, of proclamation, and of honoring.

mots.racine: ر ف ع / د ر ج
mots.def: rafīʿ: who raises; darajāt: ranks, degrees.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ر و ح
mots.def: alqā: to cast, inspire; rūḥ: spirit, revelation.
talāq: meeting (of creatures, of the Lord).

versets.ar: يَوْمَ هُم بَارِزُونَ ۖ لَا يَخْفَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ مِنْهُمْ شَيْءٌ ۚ لِّمَنِ الْمُلْكُ الْيَوْمَ ۖ لِلَّهِ الْوَاحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ
versets.tr: yawma hum bārizūna lā yakhfā ʿalā llāhi minhum shayʾun li-mani l-mulku l-yawma li-llāhi l-wāḥidi l-qahhāri
versets.trad: the Day they come forth, nothing of them hidden from God. “To whom belongs the dominion this Day?” To God, the One, the Subduer!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the divine omniscience beyond even the existent: He knows the present and the absent, what was and what will be, what will not be because He knows it cannot be, and what could have been — how it would have been had it been. On “To whom belongs the dominion this Day?”: His kingship is bound to no day nor reserved for any time; but the claims of creatures, today, are without foundation — tomorrow they fall silent and those illusions collapse.

mots.racine: و ح د / ق ه ر
mots.def: qahhār: the One who overpowers all.

versets.ar: الْيَوْمَ تُجْزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ ۚ لَا ظُلْمَ الْيَوْمَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: al-yawma tujzā kullu nafsin bimā kasabat lā ẓulma l-yawma inna llāha sarīʿu l-ḥisābi
versets.trad: This Day every soul will be recompensed for what it earned. No injustice this Day. God is swift in reckoning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lets the recompense rain down according to ranks: He rewards them for their works with the Gardens, for their states with good pleasure, for their breaths with nearness, for their love with the vision. And He rewards the sinners for their repentance with forgiveness, for their weeping with light and healing. “No injustice” means that it is impossible to ascribe to Him the slightest wrong: all that He does, He has the right to do. As for the swiftness of the reckoning with His friends, it is in the very instant: He calls them to account for the small and the great, the date-stone and the husk.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ن ف س
س ر ع / ح س ب

versets.ar: وَأَنذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْآزِفَةِ إِذِ الْقُلُوبُ لَدَى الْحَنَاجِرِ كَاظِمِينَ ۚ مَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ وَلَا شَفِيعٍ يُطَاعُ
versets.tr: wa-andhirhum yawma l-āzifati idhi l-qulūbu ladā l-ḥanājiri kāẓimīna mā li-ẓ-ẓālimīna min ḥamīmin wa-lā shafīʿin yuṭāʿu
versets.trad: And warn them of the Day of the Imminent, when hearts will be at the throats, choking. The wrongdoers will have no devoted friend, nor any intercessor to be obeyed.
versets.commentaire: On the “Day of the Imminent” (al-Āzifa), Qushayrī distinguishes two resurrections: the resurrection of all is deferred; but the resurrection of the lovers is brought forward — at every breath they have their own Day, made of chastisement and sweetness, of distancing and drawing near. And in them the limbs bear witness despite themselves: the tear testifies, the heartbeat speaks, the wasting tells, the complexion confesses; the servant veils, but the trial unveils. On the Day of the great departure, their hearts rise to their throats and their eyes choke with tears when the call to journey resounds.

mots.def: āzifa: the approaching, the imminent Hour.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ح ن ج ر
mots.def: ḥanājir: gullets; image of extreme dread.

mots.racine: ش ف ع / ط و ع
mots.def: shafīʿ: intercessor; yuṭāʿ: who is heeded.

versets.ar: يَعْلَمُ خَائِنَةَ الْأَعْيُنِ وَمَا تُخْفِي الصُّدُورُ
versets.tr: yaʿlamu khāʾinata l-aʿyuni wa-mā tukhfī ṣ-ṣudūru
versets.trad: He knows the treachery of the eyes and what the breasts conceal.
versets.commentaire: A verse Qushayrī turns toward love. The furtive glance of the lovers is to find beauty in something other than the Beloved — and he cites: “O coolness of my eye: ask my eye whether it has ever adorned itself with a fair sight since you vanished from my gaze.” The furtive glance of the gnostics is that their heart should have news of what their eye falls upon; that of the people of Unity, that a tear should flow in regret for a creature that passes away. And “what the breasts conceal,” the Real is well aware of it.

mots.racine: خ و ن / ع ي ن
خ ف ي / ص د ر
mots.def: akhfā: to hide; ṣudūr: breasts, hearts.

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ يَقْضِي بِالْحَقِّ ۖ وَالَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ لَا يَقْضُونَ بِشَيْءٍ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu yaqḍī bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-lladhīna yadʿūna min dūnihi lā yaqḍūna bi-shayʾin inna llāha huwa s-samīʿu l-baṣīru
versets.trad: And God judges with truth, while those they invoke besides Him judge nothing at all. God alone is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the divine decree in keys of love: He decrees distance for the strangers, and affection for the people of union; on the Day of arrival, He dismisses the agents of refusal. In a striking image: just as death, it is said, will be slaughtered between Paradise and the Fire in the form of a ram, it is no wonder that separation should be slaughtered at the threshold of the beloved in the figure of a detested being, that the people of the Presence may behold it. God alone “hears and sees”: no other decides.

mots.racine: ق ض ي / ح ق ق
س م ع / ب ص ر
mots.def: samīʿ: who hears; baṣīr: who sees.

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ كَانُوا مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَانُوا هُمْ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً وَآثَارًا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَمَا كَانَ لَهُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ مِن وَاقٍ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yasīrū fī l-arḍi fa-yanẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu lladhīna kānū min qablihim kānū hum ashadda minhum quwwatan wa-āthāran fī l-arḍi fa-akhadhahumu llāhu bi-dhunūbihim wa-mā kāna lahum mina llāhi min wāqin
versets.trad: Have they not journeyed through the land and seen how was the end of those before them? They were greater than them in might and in the traces they left upon the earth; yet God seized them for their sins, and they had no protector against God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transmutes the geographical journey into a threefold inner itinerary: Have they not journeyed the earth with their persons, its easts and wests, to take heed and detach themselves? Have they not journeyed with their hearts in the invisible Kingdom, by the surge of thought, to witness the lights of unveiling and gain insight? Have they not journeyed with their inmost secrets in the expanses of the Eternal-Self-Sufficient, there to be consumed under the dominion of realities and delivered from every creature, the far and the near?

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانَت تَّأْتِيهِمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَكَفَرُوا فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّهُ قَوِيٌّ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahum kānat taʾtīhim rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināti fa-kafarū fa-akhadhahumu llāhu innahu qawiyyun shadīdu l-ʿiqābi
versets.trad: That was because their messengers used to come to them with clear proofs, yet they disbelieved; so God seized them. Indeed He is Strong, severe in punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the fall of nations a warning for the disciple: if a sincere wayfarer does not reach his goal, let him know that the cause of his veiling is an objection that brushed his heart against one of his masters, in some moment; for the masters hold, for the aspirants, the place of ambassadors. And he reports: “The master among his people is like the prophet within his community.” The denial of manifest proofs has always summoned the seizing of the Strong.

mots.racine: ق و ي / ش د د

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِآيَاتِنَا وَسُلْطَانٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-laqad arsalnā mūsā bi-āyātinā wa-sulṭānin mubīnin
versets.trad: And We sent Moses with Our signs and a manifest authority,
versets.commentaire: The great account of Moses opens. Qushayrī sets the two extremes of humanity face to face: the noblest creature of his time was Moses; the basest, the most abased under His decree and the most sunken in denial, was Pharaoh — none but he dared say: “I know no god for you other than myself.” And here is the marvel of the divine design: God sends the most elect of His servants to the basest among them. The “signs” and the “manifest authority” are the weapons of light against arrogance.

mots.racine: س ل ط / ب ي ن

versets.ar: إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهَامَانَ وَقَارُونَ فَقَالُوا سَاحِرٌ كَذَّابٌ
versets.tr: ilā firʿawna wa-hāmāna wa-qārūna fa-qālū sāḥirun kadhdhābun
versets.trad: to Pharaoh, Hāmān, and Korah; but they said: “A sorcerer, a liar!”
versets.commentaire: Before the most elect of messengers, Pharaoh and his two accomplices could answer only with insult. Qushayrī: he met him with denial, ascribed sorcery to him, heaped upon him every kind of reproach. Yet God did not hasten His punishment: He granted him respite until He brought his misfortune to its term — for He is indeed forbearing toward His servants. The tyrant's reprieve is not indulgence, but the hidden completion of the decree.

mots.def: three figures of rebellious power, cunning, and wealth.

mots.racine: س ح ر / ك ذ ب

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُم بِالْحَقِّ مِنْ عِندِنَا قَالُوا اقْتُلُوا أَبْنَاءَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ وَاسْتَحْيُوا نِسَاءَهُمْ ۚ وَمَا كَيْدُ الْكَافِرِينَ إِلَّا فِي ضَلَالٍ
versets.tr: fa-lammā jāʾahum bi-l-ḥaqqi min ʿindinā qālū qtulū abnāʾa lladhīna āmanū maʿahu wa-staḥyū nisāʾahum wa-mā kaydu l-kāfirīna illā fī ḍalālin
versets.trad: And when he brought them the truth from Us, they said: “Kill the sons of those who believe with him and spare their women.” But the scheming of the disbelievers is only in error.
versets.commentaire: Pharaoh resolved to destroy Moses and his people, mustering his armies. But, says Qushayrī, it was as God said: “the scheming of the deniers is only in error,” for when one digs a pit for a friend of God, none falls into it but the very one who dug it — such is the way the Real has established. The plot against the beloved turns back, by an infallible law, upon the plotter.

mots.def: ḥaqq: truth; ʿindanā: from with Us.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ذَرُونِي أَقْتُلْ مُوسَىٰ وَلْيَدْعُ رَبَّهُ ۖ إِنِّي أَخَافُ أَن يُبَدِّلَ دِينَكُمْ أَوْ أَن يُظْهِرَ فِي الْأَرْضِ الْفَسَادَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla firʿawnu dharūnī aqtul mūsā wa-l-yadʿu rabbahu innī akhāfu an yubaddila dīnakum aw an yuẓhira fī l-arḍi l-fasāda
versets.trad: Pharaoh said: “Let me kill Moses, and let him call upon his Lord! I fear he may change your religion or spread corruption in the land.”
versets.commentaire: Pharaoh claims to fear the corruption of the land. Qushayrī unmasks the inversion: he feared that Moses would change your religion and spread evil upon the earth — when the corruptor was Pharaoh himself. It was as the proverb says: “She accused me of her own malady, then slipped away.” He schemed, but the schemer does not escape his own scheme. The tyrant projects his crime onto the innocent.

mots.racine: و ذ ر / ق ت ل

versets.ar: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ إِنِّي عُذْتُ بِرَبِّي وَرَبِّكُم مِّن كُلِّ مُتَكَبِّرٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُ بِيَوْمِ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: wa-qāla mūsā innī ʿudhtu bi-rabbī wa-rabbikum min kulli mutakabbirin lā yuʾminu bi-yawmi l-ḥisābi
versets.trad: And Moses said: “I have taken refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant one who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.”
versets.commentaire: Faced with the threat, Moses does not draw the sword: he takes refuge in God. Qushayrī makes of this recourse the model of the threatened servant: Moses sought asylum with his Lord, and it was then that there rose, to answer the tyrants, the believer of Pharaoh's household, who concealed his faith. The true bulwark against the arrogant one who denies the Day of Reckoning is not force, but the trusting refuge in the Lord of the worlds.

mots.sens: I have taken refuge in my Lord

versets.ar: وَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مُّؤْمِنٌ مِّنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَكْتُمُ إِيمَانَهُ أَتَقْتُلُونَ رَجُلًا أَن يَقُولَ رَبِّيَ اللَّهُ وَقَدْ جَاءَكُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَكُ كَاذِبًا فَعَلَيْهِ كَذِبُهُ ۖ وَإِن يَكُ صَادِقًا يُصِبْكُم بَعْضُ الَّذِي يَعِدُكُمْ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي مَنْ هُوَ مُسْرِفٌ كَذَّابٌ
versets.tr: wa-qāla rajulun muʾminun min āli firʿawna yaktumu īmānahu a-taqtulūna rajulan an yaqūla rabbiya llāhu wa-qad jāʾakum bi-l-bayyināti min rabbikum wa-in yaku kādhiban fa-ʿalayhi kadhibuhu wa-in yaku ṣādiqan yuṣibkum baʿḍu lladhī yaʿidukum inna llāha lā yahdī man huwa musrifun kadhdhābun
versets.trad: And a believing man from the family of Pharaoh, who concealed his faith, said: “Will you kill a man for saying ‘My Lord is God,’ when he has brought you clear proofs from your Lord? If he is a liar, his lie is upon him; and if he is truthful, some of what he threatens you with will strike you. God does not guide one who is a transgressor and a liar.”
versets.commentaire: Here is the sūra's discreet hero: the believer of Pharaoh's family, who veiled his faith. Qushayrī salutes his tenacious fidelity: he counseled them and pleaded against them, but no counsel, no word penetrated them. How often this believer of Pharaoh's house repeated his warnings! They did not listen to him, and it befell as the verse says: “How much advice I poured upon your tracks — and at times the well-wisher reaps only hatred.” Hidden faith does not prevent the courage of the just word.

mots.racine: ر ج ل / أ م ن
ك ت م / أ م ن
س ر ف / ك ذ ب

versets.ar: يَا قَوْمِ لَكُمُ الْمُلْكُ الْيَوْمَ ظَاهِرِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَمَن يَنصُرُنَا مِن بَأْسِ اللَّهِ إِن جَاءَنَا ۚ قَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ مَا أُرِيكُمْ إِلَّا مَا أَرَىٰ وَمَا أَهْدِيكُمْ إِلَّا سَبِيلَ الرَّشَادِ
versets.tr: yā qawmi lakumu l-mulku l-yawma ẓāhirīna fī l-arḍi fa-man yanṣurunā min baʾsi llāhi in jāʾanā qāla firʿawnu mā urīkum illā mā arā wa-mā ahdīkum illā sabīla r-rashādi
versets.trad: “O my people, yours is the dominion today, prevailing in the land; but who will help us against God's might if it comes upon us?” Pharaoh said: “I show you only what I see, and I guide you only to the path of right conduct.”
versets.commentaire: The believer recalls the precariousness of all dominion: who will save from God's wrath when it descends? Throughout this account Qushayrī underscores the mutual obstinacy: he persisted in exhorting them, and they persisted in their denial and stubbornness. Pharaoh, for his part, sets up his own gaze as the sole measure of truth — “I show you only what I see” — a perfect illustration of the soul that mistakes its blindness for clear sight.

mots.racine: س ب ل / ر ش د

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِي آمَنَ يَا قَوْمِ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُم مِّثْلَ يَوْمِ الْأَحْزَابِ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhī āmana yā qawmi innī akhāfu ʿalaykum mithla yawmi l-aḥzābi
versets.trad: And the one who believed said: “O my people, I fear for you something like the day of the factions,
versets.commentaire: The hidden believer now speaks as a shepherd of souls: his fear is not for himself but for his people. Qushayrī sets this scene within the perseverance of sincere counsel despite rejection: he warns that a fate like that of the annihilated confederates might descend upon them. It is the solicitude of the believer who, even amid tyrants, remains an intercessor and a watchman, dreading for others what the heedless do not dread for themselves.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ح ز ب
mots.sens: like the day of the factions

versets.ar: مِثْلَ دَأْبِ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَعَادٍ وَثَمُودَ وَالَّذِينَ مِن بَعْدِهِمْ ۚ وَمَا اللَّهُ يُرِيدُ ظُلْمًا لِّلْعِبَادِ
versets.tr: mithla daʾbi qawmi nūḥin wa-ʿādin wa-thamūda wa-lladhīna min baʿdihim wa-mā llāhu yurīdu ẓulman li-l-ʿibādi
versets.trad: a fate like that of the people of Noah, and ʿĀd, and Thamūd, and those after them. And God wills no injustice for His servants.
versets.commentaire: The believer lines up the thunderstruck nations — Noah, ʿĀd, Thamūd — to rouse the hearts. Qushayrī affirms forcefully that the ruin of these peoples never comes from any divine wrong: God wills no injustice toward His servants; all that befalls them proceeds from their own denial. The divine justice is such that punishment is only the ripened fruit of refusal, never the arbitrariness of an unjust Master.

mots.sens: the fate of the people of Noah

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ع ب د

versets.ar: وَيَا قَوْمِ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ يَوْمَ التَّنَادِ
versets.tr: wa-yā qawmi innī akhāfu ʿalaykum yawma t-tanādi
versets.trad: And O my people, I fear for you the Day of Mutual Calling,
versets.commentaire: The “Day of Mutual Calling” (yawm at-tanād) is the Day when creatures will call to one another in vain from shore to shore. The believer, in Qushayrī's spiritual reading, remains the watchman who dreads for his people the hour when no call will be answered. His fear is not weakness but lucidity: he already sees the tumult of the Day when each will seek a respondent and find none.

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تُوَلُّونَ مُدْبِرِينَ مَا لَكُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ مِنْ عَاصِمٍ ۗ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُ مِنْ هَادٍ
versets.tr: yawma tuwallūna mudbirīna mā lakum mina llāhi min ʿāṣimin wa-man yuḍlili llāhu fa-mā lahu min hādin
versets.trad: the Day you will turn back fleeing, with none to defend you against God. And whomever God leads astray, for him there is no guide.”
versets.commentaire: On the Day of the rout, no rampart holds against God, and none guides the one God leaves to his straying. Qushayrī, faithful to his pedagogy of hope, never forgets that this abandonment is only the sanction of an obstinate refusal; but he also recalls the absolute gravity of the decree: when the judgment seals a heart, the abundance of counsel no longer profits. Flight away from God has no exit; only flight toward God saves.

mots.racine: و ل ي / د ب ر

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ جَاءَكُمْ يُوسُفُ مِن قَبْلُ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَمَا زِلْتُمْ فِي شَكٍّ مِّمَّا جَاءَكُم بِهِ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا هَلَكَ قُلْتُمْ لَن يَبْعَثَ اللَّهُ مِن بَعْدِهِ رَسُولًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُضِلُّ اللَّهُ مَنْ هُوَ مُسْرِفٌ مُّرْتَابٌ
versets.tr: wa-laqad jāʾakum yūsufu min qablu bi-l-bayyināti fa-mā ziltum fī shakkin mimmā jāʾakum bihi ḥattā idhā halaka qultum lan yabʿatha llāhu min baʿdihi rasūlan kadhālika yuḍillu llāhu man huwa musrifun murtābun
versets.trad: And Joseph had already come to you before with clear proofs, but you remained in doubt about what he brought you. Until, when he died, you said: “God will never send a messenger after him.” Thus does God lead astray the transgressor, the doubter,
versets.commentaire: The believer invokes the memory of Joseph, who had already come with proofs and already been doubted. Qushayrī: he showed them that their denial is in every way like the denial of their fathers and forefathers of old; and just as He destroyed those long ago, so will He do with these. Doubt is transmitted like a cursed inheritance, and straying strikes “the transgressor, the doubter” who shuts upon himself the door of guidance.

mots.def: Yūsuf: the earlier prophet in Egypt.

mots.racine: س ر ف / ر ي ب
mots.def: murtāb: who doubts in bad faith.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُجَادِلُونَ فِي آيَاتِ اللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ سُلْطَانٍ أَتَاهُمْ ۖ كَبُرَ مَقْتًا عِندَ اللَّهِ وَعِندَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ قَلْبِ مُتَكَبِّرٍ جَبَّارٍ
versets.tr: alladhīna yujādilūna fī āyāti llāhi bi-ghayri sulṭānin atāhum kabura maqtan ʿinda llāhi wa-ʿinda lladhīna āmanū kadhālika yaṭbaʿu llāhu ʿalā kulli qalbi mutakabbirin jabbārin
versets.trad: those who dispute over the signs of God without any authority that came to them. Great is the loathing in the sight of God and of those who believe. Thus does God seal the heart of every arrogant tyrant.
versets.commentaire: Barren controversy over the signs of God, without true argument, draws the loathing of Heaven and of the believers. Qushayrī elsewhere links this “arrogance in the breasts” to what prevents submission to the truth, holds one far from God, and never lets one reach the sought-after goal. The seal (ṭabʿ) upon the heart of the “arrogant tyrant” is no imposed fate, but the crystallization of a freely sustained refusal.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / م ق ت
ك ب ر / ج ب ر

versets.ar: وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ يَا هَامَانُ ابْنِ لِي صَرْحًا لَّعَلِّي أَبْلُغُ الْأَسْبَابَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla firʿawnu yā hāmānu bni lī ṣarḥan laʿallī ablughu l-asbāba
versets.trad: And Pharaoh said: “O Hāmān, build me a tower that I may reach the ways,
versets.commentaire: Pharaoh orders a tower raised to “reach the ways.” Qushayrī censures this gross representation of the divine: the sabab is the means of access to a thing: he meant, “perhaps I shall reach the sky to observe the God of Moses.” And were there, between one who claims the Worshipped is in the sky and the unbeliever, only this resemblance, it would suffice for the disgrace of their doctrine. Pharaoh erred in imagining the Worshipped localized in the heights.

mots.racine: ب ن ي / ص ر ح

mots.sens: the ways, the means of access

versets.ar: أَسْبَابَ السَّمَاوَاتِ فَأَطَّلِعَ إِلَىٰ إِلَٰهِ مُوسَىٰ وَإِنِّي لَأَظُنُّهُ كَاذِبًا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ زُيِّنَ لِفِرْعَوْنَ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ وَصُدَّ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ ۚ وَمَا كَيْدُ فِرْعَوْنَ إِلَّا فِي تَبَابٍ
versets.tr: asbāba s-samāwāti fa-aṭṭaliʿa ilā ilāhi mūsā wa-innī la-aẓunnuhu kādhiban wa-kadhālika zuyyina li-firʿawna sūʾu ʿamalihi wa-ṣudda ʿani s-sabīli wa-mā kaydu firʿawna illā fī tabābin
versets.trad: the ways of the heavens, that I may look upon the God of Moses — though I think him a liar.” Thus was Pharaoh's evil deed made fair to him, and he was barred from the path; and the scheming of Pharaoh came only to ruin.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī is categorical: his belief that the Worshipped is in the sky is an error, and by it he is turned away from the path of God; for if the Worshipped were there, Pharaoh would have been right to seek Him in the sky. The making-fair (tazyīn) of the evil deed is the supreme snare: the misguided soul finds its own crime beautiful. And all Pharaoh's machination, says the verse, ends only in ruin (tabāb).

mots.racine: ط ل ع / أ ل ه
mots.sens: that I may look upon the God of Moses
mots.def: iṭṭalaʿa: to climb to view, scrutinize.

mots.racine: ز ي ن / س و أ
mots.sens: his evil deed was made fair

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِي آمَنَ يَا قَوْمِ اتَّبِعُونِ أَهْدِكُمْ سَبِيلَ الرَّشَادِ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhī āmana yā qawmi ttabiʿūni ahdikum sabīla r-rashādi
versets.trad: And the one who believed said: “O my people, follow me; I will guide you to the path of right conduct.
versets.commentaire: To the “path of right conduct” falsely claimed by Pharaoh (v.29), the believer opposes true rectitude. Qushayrī sums up the tragic tenacity of the scene: he persisted in calling them, and they persisted in their denial and stubbornness. The sage does not weary of inviting, even at humanly pure loss; for the sincere call is in itself a fidelity, independent of its visible fruit.

mots.racine: س ب ل / ر ش د

versets.ar: يَا قَوْمِ إِنَّمَا هَٰذِهِ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا مَتَاعٌ وَإِنَّ الْآخِرَةَ هِيَ دَارُ الْقَرَارِ
versets.tr: yā qawmi innamā hādhihi l-ḥayātu d-dunyā matāʿun wa-inna l-ākhirata hiya dāru l-qarāri
versets.trad: O my people, this worldly life is but a passing enjoyment, and the Hereafter is the abode of permanence.
versets.commentaire: The believer unveils the founding contrast of all wisdom: this lower world is but a brief enjoyment (matāʿ); the Hereafter alone is “the abode of stability” (dār al-qarār). Where Pharaoh is intoxicated by present power, the sage teaches detachment: the life here is only a passage, and the heart finds its stable footing only by orienting itself toward the permanent abode where no decline threatens.

mots.racine: د و ر / ق ر ر

versets.ar: مَنْ عَمِلَ سَيِّئَةً فَلَا يُجْزَىٰ إِلَّا مِثْلَهَا ۖ وَمَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُولَٰئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ يُرْزَقُونَ فِيهَا بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ
versets.tr: man ʿamila sayyiʾatan fa-lā yujzā illā mithlahā wa-man ʿamila ṣāliḥan min dhakarin aw unthā wa-huwa muʾminun fa-ulāʾika yadkhulūna l-jannata yurzaqūna fīhā bi-ghayri ḥisābin
versets.trad: Whoever does an evil deed will be recompensed only with its like; but whoever does righteousness, male or female, while a believer — those will enter Paradise, provided for therein without measure.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the merciful asymmetry of the recompense: “he will be recompensed only with its like” concerns the measure, not the quality: for the first deed is an evil, but God's recompense for it is a good, not an evil. And “while a believer” means a believer at that very instant, for from one who is not a believer the good deed does not proceed. The reward is a perpetual, everlasting provision: they will never leave Paradise nor the state they are in.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ح س ب

versets.ar: وَيَا قَوْمِ مَا لِي أَدْعُوكُمْ إِلَى النَّجَاةِ وَتَدْعُونَنِي إِلَى النَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-yā qawmi mā lī adʿūkum ilā n-najāti wa-tadʿūnanī ilā n-nāri
versets.trad: O my people, how is it that I call you to salvation while you call me to the Fire?
versets.commentaire: The believer marvels at the reversal: he calls to salvation, they call him to the Fire. Qushayrī specifies: all this is the speech of the believer of Pharaoh's family, which he utters by way of pleading before his people, binding upon them the compelling argument. The logic of disbelief is an inversion: it names the rescue fire and the fire rescue, and holds wisdom itself to be madness.

mots.racine: د ع و / ن ج و
د ع و / ن و ر
mots.sens: you call me to the Fire

versets.ar: تَدْعُونَنِي لِأَكْفُرَ بِاللَّهِ وَأُشْرِكَ بِهِ مَا لَيْسَ لِي بِهِ عِلْمٌ وَأَنَا أَدْعُوكُمْ إِلَى الْعَزِيزِ الْغَفَّارِ
versets.tr: tadʿūnanī li-akfura bi-llāhi wa-ushrika bihi mā laysa lī bihi ʿilmun wa-anā adʿūkum ilā l-ʿazīzi l-ghaffāri
versets.trad: You call me to disbelieve in God and to associate with Him that of which I have no knowledge, while I call you to the Mighty, the All-Forgiving.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the plea: you call me to deny God and to associate with Him without any proof of the soundness of your claim; and I call you to God, to what He has made clear by argument and established by demonstration. The contrast ends on two of the sūra's key Names: al-ʿAzīz (the Mighty) and al-Ghaffār (the All-Forgiving) — strength and clemency joined in the object of the call.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / غ ف ر

versets.ar: لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّمَا تَدْعُونَنِي إِلَيْهِ لَيْسَ لَهُ دَعْوَةٌ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَلَا فِي الْآخِرَةِ وَأَنَّ مَرَدَّنَا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَأَنَّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ هُمْ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ
versets.tr: lā jarama annamā tadʿūnanī ilayhi laysa lahu daʿwatun fī d-dunyā wa-lā fī l-ākhirati wa-anna maraddanā ilā llāhi wa-anna l-musrifīna hum aṣḥābu n-nāri
versets.trad: No doubt that what you call me to has no claim to be called upon in this world or the Hereafter; that our return is to God; and that the transgressors are the companions of the Fire.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī comments without detour: no doubt that what you call me to is vain: these idols have neither life, nor knowledge, nor power; they neither profit nor harm. We have learned — by the word of those whose miracles attested their truthfulness — your falsehood in what you say. The return (maradd) to God is the one certainty that exposes the nothingness of the false objects of worship.

mots.def: maradd: place and act of return.

versets.ar: فَسَتَذْكُرُونَ مَا أَقُولُ لَكُمْ ۚ وَأُفَوِّضُ أَمْرِي إِلَى اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبَادِ
versets.tr: fa-sa-tadhkurūna mā aqūlu lakum wa-ufawwiḍu amrī ilā llāhi inna llāha baṣīrun bi-l-ʿibādi
versets.trad: You will remember what I say to you. And I entrust my affair to God; indeed God is All-Seeing of His servants.”
versets.commentaire: The spiritual summit of the hidden believer: tafwīḍ, total entrusting. Qushayrī: I commit my affair to God, I rely upon Him, I fear neither you nor your scheming. To have said all, then to entrust all: such is the posture of the free man, who, having fulfilled his duty of speech, abandons the outcome to the One who “sees His servants perfectly.” The threat of men is extinguished in trust toward the Seer.

versets.ar: فَوَقَاهُ اللَّهُ سَيِّئَاتِ مَا مَكَرُوا ۖ وَحَاقَ بِآلِ فِرْعَوْنَ سُوءُ الْعَذَابِ
versets.tr: fa-waqāhu llāhu sayyiʾāti mā makarū wa-ḥāqa bi-āli firʿawna sūʾu l-ʿadhābi
versets.trad: So God shielded him from the evils of their scheming, and a dire punishment encompassed the family of Pharaoh:
versets.commentaire: The believer's trusting surrender (v.44) at once receives its answer: God shields him from the snares. It is the living illustration of the law Qushayrī stated above — whoever digs a pit for the friend of God falls into it himself: the plot recoils upon the plotter. Tafwīḍ is never disappointed: the one who has entrusted his fate to God is guarded, while the evil of the machinations encircles those who wove it.

versets.ar: النَّارُ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْهَا غُدُوًّا وَعَشِيًّا ۖ وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ أَدْخِلُوا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ أَشَدَّ الْعَذَابِ
versets.tr: an-nāru yuʿraḍūna ʿalayhā ghuduwwan wa-ʿashiyyan wa-yawma taqūmu s-sāʿatu adkhilū āla firʿawna ashadda l-ʿadhābi
versets.trad: the Fire, to which they are exposed morning and evening. And the Day the Hour comes: “Admit the family of Pharaoh to the severest punishment!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in this verse a proof of the punishment of the grave: exposure to the Fire “morning and evening” precedes the Hour. As for the “severest punishment,” he gives a moving reading for the believing sinners: the harshest torment of the Fire for the unbeliever is despair of leaving it; but for the sinners among the believers, the harshest of their punishment is when they know that this is the day of the meeting of the believers — when they grasp this, it is the most crushing of their days of pain.

mots.racine: غ د و / ع ش و
ش د د / ع ذ ب

versets.ar: وَإِذْ يَتَحَاجُّونَ فِي النَّارِ فَيَقُولُ الضُّعَفَاءُ لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا كُنَّا لَكُمْ تَبَعًا فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّغْنُونَ عَنَّا نَصِيبًا مِّنَ النَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-idh yataḥājjūna fī n-nāri fa-yaqūlu ḍ-ḍuʿafāʾu li-lladhīna stakbarū innā kunnā lakum tabaʿan fa-hal antum mughnūna ʿannā naṣīban mina n-nāri
versets.trad: And when they dispute in the Fire, the weak will say to those who were arrogant: “We were your followers; so will you relieve us of a share of the Fire?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the infernal dispute: the weak say to the arrogant: “It is you who led us astray”; and the arrogant answer them: “It is you who approved us of your own choice.” And he adds a profound intuition: their mutual quarrel only deepens the fury of their hearts; thus they are chastised by themselves, by the narrowness of their breasts, and by the hatred they bear one another. Hell is also this inner torture of resentment.

mots.def: aghnā ʿan: to spare, avail against.

versets.ar: قَالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا كُلٌّ فِيهَا إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ حَكَمَ بَيْنَ الْعِبَادِ
versets.tr: qāla lladhīna stakbarū innā kullun fīhā inna llāha qad ḥakama bayna l-ʿibādi
versets.trad: Those who were arrogant will say: “We are all in it! God has already judged between His servants.”
versets.commentaire: The chiefs confess their own helplessness: all are equally trapped. Qushayrī here stresses the constant concern for responsibility: the leaders throw back at the followers their free assent — “you approved us of your own choice.” God's judgment “between His servants” is now closed: there is no longer any recourse, no transfer of pain, each bearing the weight of his own consent to evil.

mots.racine: ح ك م / ع ب د

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ فِي النَّارِ لِخَزَنَةِ جَهَنَّمَ ادْعُوا رَبَّكُمْ يُخَفِّفْ عَنَّا يَوْمًا مِّنَ الْعَذَابِ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna fī n-nāri li-khazanati jahannama dʿū rabbakum yukhaffif ʿannā yawman mina l-ʿadhābi
versets.trad: And those in the Fire will say to the keepers of Hell: “Call upon your Lord to lighten our punishment by a single day.”
versets.commentaire: The damned ask the keepers to intercede, if only for one day. Qushayrī reads in this a sign of estrangement: this too is a mark of the state of the stranger: they place an intermediary between themselves and their Lord. Then God withdraws mercy from the hearts of the angels, so that they do not intercede for them. Their very reflex — to go through a mediator rather than implore God directly — betrays the distance in which their heart has settled. The near one prays without relay; the stranger seeks intermediaries.

mots.def: khazana: keepers, wardens of the Fire.

versets.ar: قَالُوا أَوَلَمْ تَكُ تَأْتِيكُمْ رُسُلُكُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ ۖ قَالُوا بَلَىٰ ۚ قَالُوا فَادْعُوا ۗ وَمَا دُعَاءُ الْكَافِرِينَ إِلَّا فِي ضَلَالٍ
versets.tr: qālū a-wa-lam taku taʾtīkum rusulukum bi-l-bayyināti qālū balā qālū fa-dʿū wa-mā duʿāʾu l-kāfirīna illā fī ḍalālin
versets.trad: They will say: “Did your messengers not come to you with clear proofs?” They will reply: “Yes indeed.” They will say: “Then call yourselves!” But the call of the disbelievers is only in error.
versets.commentaire: The keepers throw the damned back upon their own responsibility: the messengers had come, the proofs were given. The invocation of the disbelievers “is only in error” — not that God refuses to hear, but that their prayer, severed from faith and come too late, no longer has a path to acceptance. As Qushayrī notes elsewhere, prayer has need for its key and sincerity for its vehicle; deprived of both, it dissipates.

mots.sens: did they not come to you

mots.racine: د ع و / ك ف ر

versets.ar: إِنَّا لَنَنصُرُ رُسُلَنَا وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَيَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْأَشْهَادُ
versets.tr: innā la-nanṣuru rusulanā wa-lladhīna āmanū fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-yawma yaqūmu l-ashhādu
versets.trad: Indeed, We will help Our messengers and those who believe, in the life of this world and on the Day the witnesses arise,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the promised help far above material victory. We help them by the signs and the modes of knowledge, until they know and witness that victory and its opposite come from God, good and evil from God. Then this sublime point: the height of help is that the ally should slay the enemy of the one he defends; but when God wills his annihilation, the beloved realizes that there is in truth no enemy, that creatures are but phantoms moved by the decrees of Power — the friend of God thus has neither enemy nor friend save God.

mots.racine: ن ص ر / ر س ل

versets.ar: يَوْمَ لَا يَنفَعُ الظَّالِمِينَ مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ ۖ وَلَهُمُ اللَّعْنَةُ وَلَهُمْ سُوءُ الدَّارِ
versets.tr: yawma lā yanfaʿu ẓ-ẓālimīna maʿdhiratuhum wa-lahumu l-laʿnatu wa-lahum sūʾu d-dāri
versets.trad: the Day when the excuse of the wrongdoers will not avail them; theirs is the curse and theirs the evil abode.
versets.commentaire: Where the excuse of the unjust is vain, Qushayrī reads in the negative the promise made to the believers: the implicit sense of this discourse is that the believers do profit from their contrition; theirs is the mercy of God, theirs the fair abode — and there remains of this lower world but very little. The verse that threatens the one consoles the other: what condemns the wrongdoer saves the penitent.

mots.racine: س و أ / د و ر

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْهُدَىٰ وَأَوْرَثْنَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ الْكِتَابَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā mūsā l-hudā wa-awrathnā banī isrāʾīla l-kitāba
versets.trad: And We gave Moses guidance and bequeathed to the Children of Israel the Book,
versets.commentaire: After the drama of Pharaoh, God recalls the gift given to Moses and the inheritance of the Book left to the Children of Israel. Qushayrī, sober here, notes that the essential of Moses's story has already been set forth. Guidance (hudā) is the first grace; the transmitted Book is the lasting wake of the prophet. Where the tyrant bequeaths only ruin, the beloved of God bequeaths a light that crosses the generations.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ه د ي
و ر ث / ك ت ب

versets.ar: هُدًى وَذِكْرَىٰ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ
versets.tr: hudan wa-dhikrā li-ulī l-albābi
versets.trad: as guidance and a reminder for those of understanding.
versets.commentaire: The Book is “guidance and a reminder for those of understanding” (ulū l-albāb). The lubb, the deep intelligence, is the kernel of the heart stripped of its husk: only the one who possesses this essential heart receives the reminder as a living light, not as a dead letter. Guidance is offered to all, but it becomes an inward reminder only for the one who has known how to keep an attentive heart.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ذ ك ر

versets.ar: فَاصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنبِكَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ بِالْعَشِيِّ وَالْإِبْكَارِ
versets.tr: fa-ṣbir inna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun wa-staghfir li-dhanbika wa-sabbiḥ bi-ḥamdi rabbika bi-l-ʿashiyyi wa-l-ibkāri
versets.trad: So be patient; the promise of God is true. Ask forgiveness for your sin, and glorify the praise of your Lord evening and morning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grades patience according to faith: patience in awaiting the divine promise is in proportion to faith and certainty: whoever has the fullest and strongest belief has the most complete patience. Then this unexpected distinction: patience is of two kinds — patience in ease and patience in trial; and patience in ease is harder than in trial. For well-being often makes one forget the Benefactor, whereas trial brings one back to Him.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ذ ن ب
ع ش و / ب ك ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُجَادِلُونَ فِي آيَاتِ اللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ سُلْطَانٍ أَتَاهُمْ ۙ إِن فِي صُدُورِهِمْ إِلَّا كِبْرٌ مَّا هُم بِبَالِغِيهِ ۚ فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yujādilūna fī āyāti llāhi bi-ghayri sulṭānin atāhum in fī ṣudūrihim illā kibrun mā hum bi-bālighīhi fa-staʿidh bi-llāhi innahu huwa s-samīʿu l-baṣīru
versets.trad: Indeed, those who dispute over the signs of God without any authority that came to them — there is nothing in their breasts but arrogance which they will never attain. So seek refuge in God; He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pierces the root of the dispute: “without any authority”: without argument. “There is nothing in their breasts but arrogance”: nothing but the arrogance that prevents them from submitting to the truth, holds them far from God, and forbids them to reach their goal. Arrogance (kibr) is that opaque veil which makes the controversy perpetual and arrival impossible. The remedy is the trusting refuge (istiʿādha) with the One who hears and sees all.

mots.def: kibr: pride, swelling of the self.

versets.ar: لَخَلْقُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ خَلْقِ النَّاسِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: la-khalqu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi akbaru min khalqi n-nāsi wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: The creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than the creation of humankind, but most people do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the a fortiori argument explicit: the creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than their resurrection and recreation after they have become dust; yet these people acknowledged the creation of the heavens and the earth while denying the resurrection. The One who has done the greater can do the lesser: to deny the return after admitting the creation is a contradiction which heedlessness veils from most people.

mots.sens: the creation of the heavens and earth

versets.ar: وَمَا يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمَىٰ وَالْبَصِيرُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَلَا الْمُسِيءُ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā yastawī l-aʿmā wa-l-baṣīru wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti wa-lā l-musīʾu qalīlan mā tatadhakkarūna
versets.trad: The blind and the seeing are not equal, nor are those who believe and do righteous deeds and the evildoer. Little do you reflect.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī beautifully unfolds the pairs that are not equal: not alike are the believer and the unbeliever, nor the one chained to his passion and the one expanded by his purity, nor the one drawn to nearness and the one veiled by chastisement, nor the one raised to contemplation and the one left to his mere witnessing, nor the one favored with felicity and the one cast off in misfortune. Inner blindness and inner sight separate souls radically — and few reflect upon it.

mots.racine: ع م ي / ب ص ر
mots.def: images of the closed soul and the seeing soul.
musīʾ: the one who does wrong.

versets.ar: إِنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَآتِيَةٌ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهَا وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: inna s-sāʿata la-ātiyatun lā rayba fīhā wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: The Hour is surely coming, no doubt about it; but most people do not believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers the certainty: the time of the Reckoning is inevitable, even if the delay stretches out to its term. The length of the respite changes nothing in the reality of the Hour; it only tests faith. People's doubt is not due to the uncertainty of the event but to the apparent slowness of its coming, which lulls distracted hearts.

mots.racine: س و ع / أ ت ي

versets.ar: وَقَالَ رَبُّكُمُ ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبَادَتِي سَيَدْخُلُونَ جَهَنَّمَ دَاخِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla rabbukumu dʿūnī astajib lakum inna lladhīna yastakbirūna ʿan ʿibādatī sa-yadkhulūna jahannama dākhirīna
versets.trad: Your Lord has said: “Call upon Me, I will respond to you. Those who are too arrogant to worship Me will enter Hell in humiliation.”
versets.commentaire: A jewel-verse of the sūra. Qushayrī draws from it a superb litany on the conditions and fruits of invocation: Call upon Me by obedience, I will respond to you with reward and ranks. Call upon Me without any distraction, I will respond to you without any delay. Call upon Me with contrition, I will respond to you with favor. Call upon Me according to your capacity, I will respond by unveiling your need fulfilled. Call upon Me with petition, I will respond with gift and largesse. And he adds: every being God calls, He answers — either by what it wishes, or by what is better for it.

mots.racine: د ع و / ج و ب
mots.sens: call upon Me, I will respond

mots.racine: ك ب ر / ع ب د

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ لِتَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ وَالنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī jaʿala lakumu l-layla li-taskunū fīhi wa-n-nahāra mubṣiran inna llāha la-dhū faḍlin ʿalā n-nāsi wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yashkurūna
versets.trad: It is God who made for you the night to rest in and the day to give sight. Indeed, God is full of grace toward people, but most people are not grateful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the manners of finding night's repose: people's rest in the night is of several kinds: the heedless rest in their heedlessness, the people of love rest by the grace of their union — and what distance between the rest of heedlessness and the rest of union! Some rest in their own kind, others in the sweetness of their works; but some find no rest, neither by day nor by night: these are the people of ardent longing, forever consumed.

mots.racine: ن ه ر / ب ص ر

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ خَالِقُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ لَّا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: dhālikumu llāhu rabbukum khāliqu kulli shayʾin lā ilāha illā huwa fa-annā tuʾfakūna
versets.trad: That is God, your Lord, Creator of all things. There is no god but He. How then are you deluded?
versets.commentaire: “That is God, your Lord” — that very One, says Qushayrī in echo of the following verse, who made your rest to be with Him, your unrest for Him, your ardent longing toward Him, your love in Him, your detachment from all for Him. The Creator of all things being the One, how can man be “deluded” (tuʾfakūna) toward others? The turning away is the scandal of a reason that, acknowledging the Source of all, yet runs toward nothingness.

mots.def: ufika: to be turned away, deceived.

versets.ar: كَذَٰلِكَ يُؤْفَكُ الَّذِينَ كَانُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ يَجْحَدُونَ
versets.tr: kadhālika yuʾfaku lladhīna kānū bi-āyāti llāhi yajḥadūna
versets.trad: Thus are deluded those who used to deny the signs of God.
versets.commentaire: The turning away is no accident: it is the consequence of obstinate denial of the signs (juḥūd). The one who has grown used to rejecting God's āyāt renders himself incapable of seeing the evident, and his heart, by dint of refusal, closes to the light. The perversion of judgment is the ripened fruit of ingratitude: to deny the Creator's mark in things is to condemn oneself to understand nothing of their meaning.

mots.def: ufika: to be turned away from truth.

mots.sens: used to deny the signs of God

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ قَرَارًا وَالسَّمَاءَ بِنَاءً وَصَوَّرَكُمْ فَأَحْسَنَ صُوَرَكُمْ وَرَزَقَكُم مِّنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ ۖ فَتَبَارَكَ اللَّهُ رَبُّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī jaʿala lakumu l-arḍa qarāran wa-s-samāʾa bināʾan wa-ṣawwarakum fa-aḥsana ṣuwarakum wa-razaqakum mina ṭ-ṭayyibāti dhālikumu llāhu rabbukum fa-tabāraka llāhu rabbu l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: It is God who made for you the earth a stable abode and the sky a structure, and fashioned you and perfected your forms, and provided you with good things. That is God, your Lord; so blessed is God, Lord of the worlds!
versets.commentaire: On “He perfected your forms,” Qushayrī offers an exquisite page: He created the Throne, the Footstool, the heavens and the earths, all creatures, without addressing this discourse to them; but to us He said: “He fashioned you and perfected your forms.” Now the beautiful is not what people find beautiful, but what the Beloved finds beautiful. And this marvel: the slanderers made your image ugly in Our sight? No — your Master beautified your forms by erasing the faults from your register and inscribing good deeds in their place, for “God changes their evil deeds into good ones.”

mots.racine: ص و ر / ح س ن
mots.sens: fashioned you and perfected your forms
mots.def: ṣawwara: to form; aḥsana: to make beautiful.

versets.ar: هُوَ الْحَيُّ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ فَادْعُوهُ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ۗ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: huwa l-ḥayyu lā ilāha illā huwa fa-dʿūhu mukhliṣīna lahu d-dīna l-ḥamdu li-llāhi rabbi l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: He is the Living; there is no god but He. So call upon Him, devoting religion purely to Him. Praise be to God, Lord of the worlds!
versets.commentaire: On the Name al-Ḥayy, Qushayrī chisels a memorable formula: “He is the Living,” the One who does not die and whose grace does not run dry. Call upon Him, then, with the tongue of need (lisān al-qūt), and that with Him is never lost. To devote sincere worship to the Living alone is to pray not with the lips only but with the whole necessity of one's being — an invocation that the eternal Living gathers up and does not let go astray.

mots.def: al-Ḥayy: the Living who does not die.

mots.racine: د ع و / خ ل ص

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنِّي نُهِيتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ لَمَّا جَاءَنِيَ الْبَيِّنَاتُ مِن رَّبِّي وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أُسْلِمَ لِرَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qul innī nuhītu an aʿbuda lladhīna tadʿūna min dūni llāhi lammā jāʾaniya l-bayyinātu min rabbī wa-umirtu an uslima li-rabbi l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: Say: “I have been forbidden to worship those you call upon besides God, since clear proofs have come to me from my Lord, and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of the worlds.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paraphrases the command given to the Prophet: Say, O Muḥammad: I have been forbidden to worship what you invoke besides God, that is, I have been commanded to disavow what you worship, to turn away from what absorbs you, and to surrender entirely to the One who created me and chose me by prophethood. The twofold movement of submission: to disengage from the false objects, and to give oneself to the Lord of the worlds alone.

mots.racine: ن ه ي / ع ب د

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ مِنْ عَلَقَةٍ ثُمَّ يُخْرِجُكُمْ طِفْلًا ثُمَّ لِتَبْلُغُوا أَشُدَّكُمْ ثُمَّ لِتَكُونُوا شُيُوخًا ۚ وَمِنكُم مَّن يُتَوَفَّىٰ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلِتَبْلُغُوا أَجَلًا مُّسَمًّى وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī khalaqakum min turābin thumma min nuṭfatin thumma min ʿalaqatin thumma yukhrijukum ṭiflan thumma li-tablughū ashuddakum thumma li-takūnū shuyūkhan wa-minkum man yutawaffā min qablu wa-li-tablughū ajalan musamman wa-laʿallakum taʿqilūna
versets.trad: It is He who created you from dust, then from a drop, then from a clot, then brings you forth as a child, then that you may reach maturity, then that you may become aged — though some of you are taken earlier — and that you may reach an appointed term; perhaps you will reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī follows step by step the unfolding of human existence: from earth to drop, from drop to clot... then from your mothers' wombs to your fathers' loins in your life, then from the state of child to youth, then to old age. This ordered progression — dust, embryo, child, maturity, old age, appointed term — is itself an argument: the One who leads man through so many degrees without chance is also the One who will bring him back. “Perhaps you will reason”: the invitation to read in one's own genesis the signature of the Creator.

mots.racine: ت ر ب / ن ط ف / ع ل ق
أ ج ل / س م و

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ ۖ فَإِذَا قَضَىٰ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī yuḥyī wa-yumītu fa-idhā qaḍā amran fa-innamā yaqūlu lahu kun fa-yakūnu
versets.trad: It is He who gives life and causes death; and when He decrees a matter, He only says to it, “Be!” and it is.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers the mystery: it is He who gives life and causes death, then resurrects in the last of the two abodes. The “Be!” (kun) expresses the absolute instantaneity of the decree: between the divine will and the existence of things, no delay, no effort, no mediation. The One who creates and causes death by a single word needs no secondary cause; the whole of creation rests in the sovereign swiftness of His word.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: He gives life and causes death
mots.def: aḥyā: to give life; amāta: to cause to die.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ يُجَادِلُونَ فِي آيَاتِ اللَّهِ أَنَّىٰ يُصْرَفُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna yujādilūna fī āyāti llāhi annā yuṣrafūna
versets.trad: Have you not seen those who dispute over the signs of God — how they are turned away?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the impasse of the contenders: before the signs of God they grow dull: they produce no argument and ward off from themselves no chastisement; they will know when their knowledge no longer avails, and they will excuse themselves when their excuse is no longer heard. The “how they are turned away” (annā yuṣrafūna) marks the astonishment before beings who, for want of proof, flee the truth toward nowhere, until the hour when it is too late.

mots.def: ṣurifa: to be diverted, turned aside.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِالْكِتَابِ وَبِمَا أَرْسَلْنَا بِهِ رُسُلَنَا ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna kadhdhabū bi-l-kitābi wa-bimā arsalnā bihi rusulanā fa-sawfa yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Those who denied the Book and what We sent Our messengers with — they will come to know,
versets.commentaire: The denial of the Book and the messages carries within it its own deferred sentence: “they will come to know.” Qushayrī insists on this tragic knowledge that comes too late, when their knowledge will no longer profit them. The verse suspends the threat in the future (sawfa): the respite is not forgetting, but the postponement of an inevitable unveiling, where the veil of denial will be torn under the weight of the evidence.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / ك ت ب

versets.ar: إِذِ الْأَغْلَالُ فِي أَعْنَاقِهِمْ وَالسَّلَاسِلُ يُسْحَبُونَ
versets.tr: idhi l-aghlālu fī aʿnāqihim wa-s-salāsilu yusḥabūna
versets.trad: when the shackles are around their necks, and the chains, as they are dragged
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the scene of terror: they are dragged into the Fire, the shackles on their necks, then made to taste the varieties of chastisement. The shackle (ghill) and the chain (silsila) figure the total enslavement of the soul that had thought itself free in its refusal: what it knotted around its heart by arrogance becomes, on the Day of unveiling, very real fetters. The freedom refused to God turns into servitude to chastisement.

mots.racine: غ ل ل / ع ن ق
س ل س ل / س ح ب

versets.ar: فِي الْحَمِيمِ ثُمَّ فِي النَّارِ يُسْجَرُونَ
versets.tr: fī l-ḥamīmi thumma fī n-nāri yusjarūna
versets.trad: into the boiling water, then in the Fire they are kindled.
versets.commentaire: The boiling water (ḥamīm) then the kindling (sajr) mark the gradation of the torment Qushayrī evokes as “the varieties of chastisement.” The contrast with the blessed water that gives life, elsewhere in the Qurʾān, is striking: here the life-giving element turns into an instrument of pain. For the deviated heart, what should have quenched now burns; the whole order of blessings is inverted for the one who inverted the meaning of creation.

mots.def: sajara: to stoke, cast into fire.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: thumma qīla lahum ayna mā kuntum tushrikūna
versets.trad: Then it will be said to them: “Where are those you used to associate
versets.commentaire: Then comes the cutting question: where are the associates? Qushayrī sets this question within the sequence of chastisement: after the dragging and the fire, the unveiling of the vanity of the false divinities. The question awaits no answer: it is itself a part of the punishment, for it forces the soul to behold the vanishing of all it had wagered on apart from God. The summoned idols do not respond.

versets.ar: مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ ۖ قَالُوا ضَلُّوا عَنَّا بَل لَّمْ نَكُن نَّدْعُو مِن قَبْلُ شَيْئًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُضِلُّ اللَّهُ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: min dūni llāhi qālū ḍallū ʿannā bal lam nakun nadʿū min qablu shayʾan kadhālika yuḍillu llāhu l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: besides God?” They will say: “They have abandoned us; rather, we used to call upon nothing before.” Thus does God lead the disbelievers astray.
versets.commentaire: A terrible admission: “we used to call upon nothing before.” The idols have vanished, and the damned recognize that they had worshipped only a nothingness. Qushayrī sees in this straying (iḍlāl) the consequence of a choice: God leaves to their ruin those who have preferred the void to the Fullness. The cult of nothing leads logically to nothing: those who prayed to the non-existent find themselves empty-handed on the Day when only the Existent remains.

mots.def: admission of the vanity of past worship.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَفْرَحُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ وَبِمَا كُنتُمْ تَمْرَحُونَ
versets.tr: dhālikum bimā kuntum tafraḥūna fī l-arḍi bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi wa-bimā kuntum tamraḥūna
versets.trad: That is because you used to exult on the earth without right, and because you were insolent.
versets.commentaire: The chastisement is tied to a precise cause: disordered joy and insolence (maraḥ) upon the earth. Qushayrī, in his comment on the sūra's close, shows these peoples drawn into the snares of their hopes, fallen into the abyss of their illusion, deceived by their impunity during the respite We granted them. Rejoicing “without truth” is that intoxication with the ephemeral which makes one forget the term — a hollow exultation paid for at the awakening.

mots.racine: ف ر ح / ح ق ق

versets.ar: ادْخُلُوا أَبْوَابَ جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ فَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ
versets.tr: udkhulū abwāba jahannama khālidīna fīhā fa-biʾsa mathwā l-mutakabbirīna
versets.trad: Enter the gates of Hell to abide therein forever; how wretched is the abode of the arrogant!
versets.commentaire: The order falls: to enter through the gates of Hell forever. Qushayrī sums up the sequence: when they have acknowledged their disbelief and their sins, it is said to them: enter the gates of Hell, abiding therein forever; how wretched their abode and their destiny, how dire their departure and their road. The “abode of the arrogant” (mathwā l-mutakabbirīn) seals the destiny of arrogance: the swelling of the self, which sought to rise, ends walled up in the lowest of places.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ب و ب
ث و ي / ك ب ر

versets.ar: فَاصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۚ فَإِمَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ الَّذِي نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ فَإِلَيْنَا يُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ṣbir inna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun fa-immā nuriyannaka baʿḍa lladhī naʿiduhum aw natawaffayannaka fa-ilaynā yurjaʿūna
versets.trad: So be patient; the promise of God is true. Whether We show you some of what We promise them, or take you in death, to Us they will be returned.
versets.commentaire: A renewal of the command to patience, with a direct consolation. Qushayrī addresses the Prophet in God's name: have your heart free of them, then look upon what will become of them; be certain that there is no duration for the tumult of their falsehood. Whether you see in your lifetime a part of what We threaten them with, or not, do not doubt that they will endure it thereafter. Whatever happens — vision or death — the outcome is guaranteed: “to Us they will be returned.”

mots.racine: ر أ ي / و ع د
mots.sens: We show you some of what We promise them
mots.def: arā: to show; waʿada: to promise/threaten.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلًا مِّن قَبْلِكَ مِنْهُم مَّن قَصَصْنَا عَلَيْكَ وَمِنْهُم مَّن لَّمْ نَقْصُصْ عَلَيْكَ ۗ وَمَا كَانَ لِرَسُولٍ أَن يَأْتِيَ بِآيَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۚ فَإِذَا جَاءَ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ قُضِيَ بِالْحَقِّ وَخَسِرَ هُنَالِكَ الْمُبْطِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad arsalnā rusulan min qablika minhum man qaṣaṣnā ʿalayka wa-minhum man lam naqṣuṣ ʿalayka wa-mā kāna li-rasūlin an yaʾtiya bi-āyatin illā bi-idhni llāhi fa-idhā jāʾa amru llāhi quḍiya bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-khasira hunālika l-mubṭilūna
versets.trad: And We sent messengers before you; of some We have told you, and of some We have not told you. No messenger could bring a sign except by God's permission. So when God's command comes, it is decided in truth, and there the falsifiers lose.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī illumines the condition of the miracle: none could bring a sign unless We made it appear upon him, when We willed. So if they demand a sign of you, know that We have already made appear upon you signs enough to lift every excuse and establish the soundness of the matter; as for what they demand, if We will We manifest it, and if We will We leave it. The sign depends not on the messenger's whim but on the divine permission (idhn) alone.

mots.sens: some of whom We have told you

mots.racine: خ س ر / ب ط ل

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَنْعَامَ لِتَرْكَبُوا مِنْهَا وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī jaʿala lakumu l-anʿāma li-tarkabū minhā wa-minhā taʾkulūna
versets.trad: It is God who made for you the cattle, that you may ride some of them and eat of some.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a memorial of grace: He reminds them of the immensity of His favor through the subjection of the cattle: He made them for you, that you might profit by them through riding, transport, work, drinking their milk, eating their flesh, using their wool and hair. And he concludes in God's name: it is I who made all this easy for you, I who inspired you to profit from it — so have confidence in it and gratitude.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ن ع م

versets.ar: وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَنَافِعُ وَلِتَبْلُغُوا عَلَيْهَا حَاجَةً فِي صُدُورِكُمْ وَعَلَيْهَا وَعَلَى الْفُلْكِ تُحْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lakum fīhā manāfiʿu wa-li-tablughū ʿalayhā ḥājatan fī ṣudūrikum wa-ʿalayhā wa-ʿalā l-fulki tuḥmalūna
versets.trad: And in them are benefits for you, that you may reach by them a need in your breasts; and upon them and upon ships you are carried.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues the inventory of blessings: to cross long distances upon them... upon the cattle and upon the vessels you move from one region to another. The symmetry between the living mount and the ship expresses the breadth of providence: on land and on sea, man is borne by what God has subjected to him. To recognize that this carrying is not owed but given is to pass from mere use to gratitude.

mots.racine: ح و ج / ص د ر
ف ل ك / ح م ل
mots.def: fulk: vessels; ḥumila: to be carried.

versets.ar: وَيُرِيكُمْ آيَاتِهِ فَأَيَّ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ تُنكِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yurīkum āyātihi fa-ayya āyāti llāhi tunkirūna
versets.trad: And He shows you His signs; so which of God's signs do you deny?
versets.commentaire: After the enumeration of blessings, the rhetorical question: which sign will you dare deny? Throughout this passage Qushayrī presents the blessing as an unveiling (yurīkum āyātahu): each grace received is a sign pointing to the Giver. Before so many marks, denial becomes indefensible; to deny one of these signs is to deny the very hand that lavishes them. Gratitude imposes itself on whoever will only look.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / أ ي ي

mots.sens: which sign of God do you deny

versets.ar: أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَانُوا أَكْثَرَ مِنْهُمْ وَأَشَدَّ قُوَّةً وَآثَارًا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَمَا أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-lam yasīrū fī l-arḍi fa-yanẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu lladhīna min qablihim kānū akthara minhum wa-ashadda quwwatan wa-āthāran fī l-arḍi fa-mā aghnā ʿanhum mā kānū yaksibūna
versets.trad: Have they not journeyed through the land and seen how was the end of those before them? They were more numerous than them, greater in might and in the traces upon the earth; yet what they earned availed them nothing.
versets.commentaire: A return to the call of the instructive journey. Qushayrī: He commands them to take heed of those who preceded them: they were stronger, richer, longer-lived; but they let themselves be drawn into the snares of their hopes and fell into the abyss of their illusion. All they had amassed — power, traces, goods — “availed them nothing” on the day of the seizing. Strength and wealth do not ransom the soul from the decree.

mots.racine: ع ق ب / ق ب ل
mots.sens: the end of those before them

mots.def: aghnā ʿan: to be of any use.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَرِحُوا بِمَا عِندَهُم مِّنَ الْعِلْمِ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā jāʾathum rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināti fariḥū bimā ʿindahum mina l-ʿilmi wa-ḥāqa bihim mā kānū bihi yastahziʾūna
versets.trad: When their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they exulted in the knowledge they possessed; but that which they mocked encompassed them.
versets.commentaire: A profound verse: they rejoiced in their own knowledge and mocked the messengers — and the mockery surrounded them. Qushayrī shows these people deceived by their security during the respite granted, proud of a knowledge that blinded them to the essential. Self-contentment (faraḥ bi-mā ʿindahum) is the snare of the arrogant scholar: he takes his partial knowledge for a sufficiency and turns into derision the truth that surpasses him — until it engulfs him.

mots.racine: ف ر ح / ع ل م
mots.def: fariḥa: to rejoice, to be self-pleased.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنَا قَالُوا آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَحْدَهُ وَكَفَرْنَا بِمَا كُنَّا بِهِ مُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā raʾaw baʾsanā qālū āmannā bi-llāhi waḥdahu wa-kafarnā bimā kunnā bihi mushrikīna
versets.trad: And when they saw Our might, they said: “We believe in God alone, and we reject what we used to associate with Him.”
themes: the faith of the last hour
versets.commentaire: At the sight of the punishment, they at last proclaim the Oneness and reject their associates. But this faith comes at the hour when it no longer saves. Qushayrī, commenting on the sūra's close, shows these people who, seeing the violence of the might and fallen into the humiliation of disappointment and despair, wished to return to the world from the beginning — a vain wish. Faith wrung out by terror is no longer the free surge of the heart, but the surrender of a broken will.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ب أ س
أ م ن / و ح د

versets.ar: فَلَمْ يَكُ يَنفَعُهُمْ إِيمَانُهُمْ لَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنَا ۖ سُنَّتَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي قَدْ خَلَتْ فِي عِبَادِهِ ۖ وَخَسِرَ هُنَالِكَ الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lam yaku yanfaʿuhum īmānuhum lammā raʾaw baʾsanā sunnata llāhi llatī qad khalat fī ʿibādihi wa-khasira hunālika l-kāfirūna
versets.trad: But their faith did not avail them once they saw Our might. Such is the way of God which has passed among His servants; and there the disbelievers lost.
versets.commentaire: The sūra is sealed on the immutable sunna of God: the faith of the last hour does not avail. Qushayrī: when they saw the violence of the chastisement and fell into the lowliness of despair, they wished to be returned to the world — but God met them with disappointment and strung them into the rank of those He had annihilated among the people of association and disgrace. And he gives the reason: repentance no longer has a place after the coming of compelling knowledge and the sight of the punishment, for its time is then past. The door of return stays open as long as the veil endures; it closes the moment the veil falls.

mots.racine: ن ف ع / أ م ن
mots.sens: their faith did not avail them

mots.def: sunna: constant law of the divine acting.

mots.racine: خ س ر / ك ف ر
mots.def: khasira: to be a loser, ruined.
